---
title : "Figure 7.3"
subtitle : "Comparison of kernels and target smoothness"
author : "Thimoté Dupuch"
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme:
      light: zephyr
      dark: darkly
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg
Pkg.activate("../..")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
using LinearAlgebra, Random, Statistics, CairoMakie
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
blue, orange, green, pink, teal, red, yellow = Makie.wong_colors()
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function get_kernel(X1, X2, kernel_type, alphak)
    D = (X1 .- X2').^2
    if kernel_type == 1       # Exponential (s=1)
        return exp.(-sqrt.(D) * alphak)
    elseif kernel_type == 2   # Matern (s=2)
        return (1.0 .+ sqrt.(D) * alphak) .* exp.(-sqrt.(D) * alphak)
    elseif kernel_type == 4   # Gaussian
        return exp.(-D * alphak * 3.0)
    end
end

function get_y(X, idata, std_noise, rng)
    if idata == 1
        return sin.(4.0 * pi * X) + std_noise * randn(rng, length(X))
    else
        return sign.(sin.(4.0 * pi * X)) + std_noise * randn(rng, length(X))
    end
end

function get_y_test(X, idata)
    if idata == 1
        return sin.(4.0 * pi * X)
    else
        return sign.(sin.(4.0 * pi * X))
    end
end

function affine_fit(x, y)
    X = hcat(x, ones(length(x)))
    theta = X \ y
    return theta[1], theta[2]
end

function run_kernel_simulations(kernel_type)
    rng = MersenneTwister(1)
    ns = round.(Int, 2.0 .^ (2:0.25:10))
    nrep = 20
    lambdas = 10.0 .^ (2:-0.25:-12)
    std_noise = 0.2

    # We will accumulate excess risks for each idata
    # valtests: (lambda_idx, n_idx, rep_idx, idata)
    valtests = zeros(length(lambdas), length(ns), nrep, 2)

    for idata in 1:2
        for irep in 1:nrep
            n_max = maximum(ns)
            ntest = n_max * 4

            Xfull = rand(rng, n_max)
            Xtest = collect(0:ntest-1) ./ (ntest - 1)
            yfull = get_y(Xfull, idata, std_noise, rng)
            ytest = get_y_test(Xtest, idata)

            for (in_idx, n) in enumerate(ns)
                X = Xfull[1:n]
                y = yfull[1:n]

                alphak = 2.0
                K = get_kernel(X, X, kernel_type, alphak)
                Ktest = get_kernel(Xtest, X, kernel_type, alphak)

                F = eigen(K)
                u = F.vectors
                e = F.values

                uy = u' * y

                for (ilambda, lambda) in enumerate(lambdas)
                    alpha = u * ( (1.0 ./ (e .+ n * lambda)) .* uy )
                    ytest_pred = Ktest * alpha
                    valtests[ilambda, in_idx, irep, idata] = mean((ytest_pred .- ytest).^2)
                end
            end
        end
    end

    # Also generate a single fit for n = 128
    n_fit = 128
    ntest_fit = 512
    X_fit = rand(rng, n_fit)
    Xtest_fit = collect(0:ntest_fit-1) ./ (ntest_fit - 1)

    fits = []
    for idata in 1:2
        y_fit = get_y(X_fit, idata, std_noise, rng)
        ytest_fit = get_y_test(Xtest_fit, idata)

        K_fit = get_kernel(X_fit, X_fit, kernel_type, 2.0)
        Ktest_fit = get_kernel(Xtest_fit, X_fit, kernel_type, 2.0)

        F = eigen(K_fit)
        u = F.vectors
        e = F.values

        valtest_fit = zeros(length(lambdas))
        uy = u' * y_fit
        for (ilambda, lambda) in enumerate(lambdas)
            alpha = u * ( (1.0 ./ (e .+ n_fit * lambda)) .* uy )
            ytest_pred = Ktest_fit * alpha
            valtest_fit[ilambda] = mean((ytest_pred .- ytest_fit).^2)
        end

        _, best_lambda_idx = findmin(valtest_fit)
        best_lambda = lambdas[best_lambda_idx]

        alpha = u * ( (1.0 ./ (e .+ n_fit * best_lambda)) .* uy )
        ytest_pred = Ktest_fit * alpha
        push!(fits, (X = X_fit, y = y_fit, Xtest = Xtest_fit, ytest = ytest_fit, ypred = ytest_pred))
    end

    return ns, valtests, fits
end
```

```{julia}
# Run simulation for all three kernel types
ns, valtests1, fits1 = run_kernel_simulations(1)
_, valtests2, fits2 = run_kernel_simulations(2)
_, valtests4, fits4 = run_kernel_simulations(4)
```

```{julia}
fig = Figure(size=(1500, 1500), fontsize=18)

# Helper function to plot a row
function plot_row!(fig, row_idx, valtests, fits, kernel_title)
    # Col 1: Smooth target
    ax1 = Axis(fig[row_idx, 1], xlabel="x", ylabel="y", title="$kernel_title - Smooth target", limits=((0, 1), (-1.5, 2.0)))
    scatter!(ax1, fits[1].X, fits[1].y, color=:black, marker=:x, markersize=8)
    lines!(ax1, fits[1].Xtest, fits[1].ytest, color=red, linewidth=2, label="target")
    lines!(ax1, fits[1].Xtest, fits[1].ypred, color=:black, linewidth=2, label="prediction")
    axislegend(ax1, position=:rt)

    # Col 2: Non-smooth target
    ax2 = Axis(fig[row_idx, 2], xlabel="x", ylabel="y", title="$kernel_title - Non-smooth target", limits=((0, 1), (-1.5, 2.0)))
    scatter!(ax2, fits[2].X, fits[2].y, color=:black, marker=:x, markersize=8)
    lines!(ax2, fits[2].Xtest, fits[2].ytest, color=red, linewidth=2, label="target")
    lines!(ax2, fits[2].Xtest, fits[2].ypred, color=:black, linewidth=2, label="prediction")
    axislegend(ax2, position=:rt)

    # Col 3: Rates
    ax3 = Axis(fig[row_idx, 3], xlabel="log2(n)", ylabel="log2(excess risk)", title="$kernel_title - Rates", limits=((2, 10), (-11, 0.5)))

    # Mean over replications, then min over lambdas
    risk_smooth = [minimum(mean(valtests[:, in_idx, :, 1], dims=2)) for in_idx in 1:length(ns)]
    risk_nonsmooth = [minimum(mean(valtests[:, in_idx, :, 2], dims=2)) for in_idx in 1:length(ns)]

    x_log = log2.(ns)
    y_smooth_log = log2.(risk_smooth)
    y_nonsmooth_log = log2.(risk_nonsmooth)

    lines!(ax3, x_log, y_smooth_log, color=blue, linewidth=2, label="smooth target")
    lines!(ax3, x_log, y_nonsmooth_log, color=red, linewidth=2, label="non-smooth target")

    a_s, b_s = affine_fit(x_log, y_smooth_log)
    a_n, b_n = affine_fit(x_log, y_nonsmooth_log)

    lines!(ax3, x_log, a_s .* x_log .+ b_s, color=blue, linestyle=:dash, linewidth=2)
    lines!(ax3, x_log, a_n .* x_log .+ b_n, color=red, linestyle=:dash, linewidth=2)

    axislegend(ax3, position=:lb)
end

plot_row!(fig, 1, valtests1, fits1, "Exponential (s=1)")
plot_row!(fig, 2, valtests2, fits2, "Matern (s=2)")
plot_row!(fig, 3, valtests4, fits4, "Gaussian")

fig
```
